---
title: "Customer Documents Roadmap"
subtitle: "Authoring backlog and current-state status for the customer-documents tree"
doc-type: planning
canon-source: "src/uiao/canon/"
derived-from: "uiao canon"
aspirational: true
---

::: {.callout-note}
This roadmap tracks authoring work in `docs/customer-documents/`. The
**Current state** section below is regenerated from the tree by
`scripts/generate_customer_docs_status.py` and gated in CI by
`.github/workflows/customer-docs-status.yml` — it cannot drift from
reality. Everything outside that section is hand-authored narrative
(open gaps, the authoring contract, sequencing context).
:::

## Current state

The status grid below is regenerated from `docs/customer-documents/**/*.qmd`
on every run of `scripts/generate_customer_docs_status.py`. Classifications
are line-count proxies for authoring depth:

| Class | Lines | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| **Stub** | ≤ 40 | Frontmatter + placeholder; needs body |
| **Brief** | 41–99 | Short landing page or one-page brief |
| **Authored** | 100–249 | Full document body |
| **Substantial** | ≥ 250 | Long-form (whitepaper, narrative, runbook) |
| **Navigation hub** | any | `index.qmd` — intentionally thin nav surface |

Section `index.qmd` files are not authoring backlog. They are deliberately
thin nav surfaces; treating them as stubs was the original bug in the
hand-maintained roadmap.

{{< include ../data/customer-documents-status.md >}}

## Open canonical gaps

These items are tracked here because the generator cannot detect them
from file shape alone. Add to this list as new gaps are discovered;
delete entries when resolved.

_No open gaps._ Both items from the 2026-05-18 baseline are closed:
the `boundary-impact-model.qmd` canon-anchor was confirmed to resolve
(`canon-source: docs/docs/gcc-boundary-solution-architecture.qmd`,
plus three valid `canon-reference:` entries), and the Phase 5
`_template/mts-generic-template.md` requirement was dropped — all six
domain MTS pages were authored independently without needing a shared
template, and a future 7th domain can re-introduce the template then
if useful.

## Sections out of original scope

These directories grew in the tree after the original Phase 1–7 plan and
are not part of any phased authoring schedule. They appear in the
**Current state** table above for visibility but have no authoring
backlog of their own — only drift watch.

- `compliance/` — controls-testing, federal-mandates, evidence-telemetry
- `modernization/` — modernization program narrative + sub-programs
- `orgpath-narrative/` — OrgPath/OrgTree concept narrative
- `platform/` — platform-server-build, git-server-implementation
- `substrate/` — core-architecture, execution-patterns, platform-tooling

## Authoring contract

Every new customer-document page must:

1. Carry canonical YAML frontmatter (`metadata-validator` CI gate).
2. Cite its `canon-source` in frontmatter.
3. Include an `aspirational: true` flag if the described capability is TARGET
   or DESIGN-ONLY per [Substrate Status](../docs/substrate-status.html).
4. Pass `link-check` (no broken internal or external references).
5. Reference images via the
   [Nano Banana pipeline](../../scripts/generate_images.py) —
   prompts go in `docs/data/image-registry.yaml`, PNGs are committed,
   and the integrity check at the bottom of the **Current state** table
   will flag any `.qmd` whose `![](images/...)` reference does not
   resolve on disk.
6. Land in the same PR as any required updates to the parent section's
   `index.qmd` — remove the slug from any "Planned pages" table once
   authored.

## Tracking new authoring work

File individual GitHub issues per page under label
`customer-docs-authoring`. Close when the page is merged into `main` and
appears in the **Current state** table above with the desired
classification (typically Authored or Substantial).

## Keeping this roadmap current

The **Current state** include block is regenerated from the tree, not
hand-edited. To refresh after adding, renaming, or removing
customer-documents:

```bash
python scripts/generate_customer_docs_status.py \
    --output docs/data/customer-documents-status.md
```

The CI workflow `.github/workflows/customer-docs-status.yml` re-runs the
generator on every PR that touches `docs/customer-documents/**` and fails
if the committed `docs/data/customer-documents-status.md` does not match
the regenerated output. To resolve a failing run, run the command above
locally and commit the updated file.

::: {.callout-tip}
The integrity table at the bottom of the **Current state** include is
where the generator surfaces broken image references and missing
frontmatter. Treat that table as a per-PR punch list — if it lists any
rows, fix them before merging.
:::

## Original phased plan (historical)

The 2026-04-23 ROADMAP organized authoring as Phases 1–7. All seven
phases landed in the tree by 2026-05; the per-phase **Stub / Partial /
Authored** tables previously kept here are no longer load-bearing
(every "Stub" in those tables is now an authored document with full
prose, tables, and diagrams). The current state of each file is
visible in the auto-generated grid above.

The phased ordering remains useful as a sequencing reference for
follow-on work, and is preserved here in summary form:

| Phase | Section | Sequencing rationale |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `executive-briefs/` | Customer-facing entry; cite from everything downstream |
| 2 | `architecture-series/` | Conceptual depth behind the briefs |
| 3 | `whitepapers/` | Long-form synthesis citing 1 + 2 |
| 4 | `executive-governance-series/` | Chapter-style governance narrative |
| 5 | `modernization-specs/` | Cross-adapter domain specs |
| 6 | `validation-suites/domains/` | Paired with Phase 5 |
| 7 | `case-studies/` | Requires shipped or late-stage capability |

_(Last hand-edited 2026-05-18 to replace the hand-maintained Phase 1–7
status tables with the regenerable **Current state** include block.
Prior baseline 2026-05-10 understated authored content because every
"Stub" labeled file had already been written to 100+ lines without the
corresponding ROADMAP refresh.)_
